text.content:
The Compendium Index is an app used to browse, filter and display compendia for the Shadow of the Weird Wizard system.

A shortcut to the app can be found by everyone in the Compendium Packs tab, and Sages can find many handy presets displayed in the Sage Tools app.

In addition to these places, the Compendium Index can be accessed through enriched text anywhere where enriched text exists:

  • Journal entries (like this documentation!)

  • Chat messages

  • Fields in actor and item sheets where the text editor is enabled, such as the Description fields.

  • Any other place where the text is enriched.

Here is a default Compendium Index enricher result:

@Index[all]

In addition to simply opening the Compendium Index, there are a few presets ready that open it with preset filters and a view on.

Below is the code and enrichment result for each available compendium index preset.

Notes that the enricher is case insensitive, meaning it does not matter if you prefer @index or @InDeX.

Equipment

Code

Result

@Index​[equipment]

@Index[equipment]

@Index​[armor]

@Index[armor]

@Index​[weapons]

@Index[weapons]

@Index​[hirelings]

@Index[hirelings]

Character Options

Code

Result

@Index​[ancestries]

@Index[ancestries]

@Index​[professions]

@Index[professions]

@Index​[novice]

@Index[novice]

@Index​[expert]

@Index[expert]

@Index​[master]

@Index[master]

@Index​[traditions]

@Index[traditions]

Other

Code

Result

@Index​[all]

@Index[all]

@Index​[creatures]

@Index[creatures]

@Index​[talents]

@Index[talents]

@Index​[‌‌spells]

@Index[spells]

In addition to presets, you can instead use the advanced options below.

You must, though, keep the syntax @Index​[param1="value1" param2="value2.1, value 2.2"].

Note that the values for parameters must be enclosed by quotes ("). While the enricher might work in some cases, it's recommended to always use quotes as best practice.

Available Parameters

Parameter and Aliases

Explanation and Example

Possible Values and Examples

label

The text shown on the widget.

If none is assigned, either the preset's label will be used or "Compendium Index" will show.

Weird Compendium!

tooltip

The tooltip when the widget is hovered.

If none is assigned, the system will use the label to come up with a name.

Very lengthy description
worth of a hover tooltip.

view

The View the app should open with.

Default: generic

Equipment: equipment, armor, weapons

Character Options: ancestries, paths, professions, traditions

Other: creatures, spells, talents (traits included)

sourceCompendia

source

compendia

The compendium packs that should be used in the Source Compendia filter.

weirdwizard.armor

weirdwizard.hirelings

weirdwizard.character-options

documentTypes

types

docTypes

The filter that determines what Document Types should be displayed by the app.

Actors: character, npc, vehicle

Items: equipment, talent, spell

Journal Entry Pages: ancestry, path, profession, tradition

Examples

Below are a few code examples and the result.

Note that in the example below, the parameters are broken down into multiple lines on the code preview!

This should not be the case for the real code! If you copy an example code and it does not work, it's probably because you have line breaks.

Code

Result

@Index​[view="equipment"
source="weirdwizard.armor,weirdwizard.weapons"
types="equipment,armor,weapons"]

@Index[view="equipment" source="weirdwizard.armor,weirdwizard.weapons" types="equipment,armor,weapons"]

@Index​[label="Fancy Label"
tooltip="Cool tooltip, brother."]

@Index[label="Fancy Label" tooltip="Cool tooltip, brother."]
